texto: I envy these people. Wide-open suffering, their messes all hanging out. Lives boiled down to raw need--a near-holiness to it. And all of us driving our cars up and down the mountain--we'll go on forever trying to fool each other.
explicacao: Citação de Jamie Quatro sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “I envy these people. Wide-open suffering, their messes all hanging out. Lives boiled down to raw …” articula vida, envy, openness em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como vida e openness.

frase_original: I envy these people. Wide-open suffering, their messes all hanging out. Lives boiled down to raw need--a near-holiness to it. And all of us driving our cars up and down the mountain--we'll go on forever trying to fool each other.

texto: In the support group, the counselor had said: When you lose a loved one, you feel as if you're inside a confined space. Everyone else will seem to be careening along outside of this space. In time, you will become aware of an opening you are going to have to step through. It might be the touch of a new lover, a new job, a move--but you'll know. You will step through.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jamie Quatro: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “In the support group, the counselor had said: When you lose a loved one, you feel as if you're in…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, grief, loss pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e loss.

frase_original: In the support group, the counselor had said: When you lose a loved one, you feel as if you're inside a confined space. Everyone else will seem to be careening along outside of this space. In time, you will become aware of an opening you are going to have to step through. It might be the touch of a new lover, a new job, a move--but you'll know. You will step through.
